# Add Board Item

Create a new item in the `board/` project management system. The system spec is bundled with the plugin at `/Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/spec/board-system-spec.md`.

Before creating, check if a similar item already exists. If a close match is found, ask the user if they still want to create a new one.

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

## Create the File

Use the correct directory, frontmatter, and naming convention:

| Type | Directory | Filename Example |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| task | `board/tasks/` | `TASK-0014_add-batch-processing.md` |
| epic | `board/planning/` | `EPIC-0009_batch-mode.md` |
| adr | `board/planning/` | `ADR-0002_chose-webgpu-over-wasm.md` |
| note | `board/planning/` | `NOTE-0001_explore-wasm-option.md` |
| reference | `board/reference/` | `REFE-0007_webgpu-overview.md` |
| resource | `board/resources/` | `RESO-0001_webgpu-article.md` |

## Frontmatter Templates

Task:
```yaml
---
type: task
status: backlog
priority: 2
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Epic:
```yaml
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

ADR:
```yaml
---
type: adr
status: active
context_date: YYYY-MM-DD
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Note:
```yaml
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Reference:
```yaml
---
type: reference
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Resource (ask user for URL):
```yaml
---
type: resource
status: inbox
url: <url>
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

## Naming Rules

- Convert title to kebab-case for filename
- Task titles MUST be imperative mood (start with a verb). If the user gives a noun phrase, rewrite it (e.g., "batch processing" -> "Add batch processing support")
- Use today's date for `created`

## After Creation

Show the file path and ask if the user wants to add more detail to the body.

```md
# Server-side rendering for crawlers and LLMs

All page content is currently rendered client-side, so crawlers and LLMs see an empty shell. We need SSR/SSG for the home page (the only page that exists).

## Recommended approach

[`vite-react-ssg`](https://vite-react-ssg.netlify.app/docs/Getting-Started) — light lift, stays within the existing Vite + React stack. Pre-renders the home page at build time so the HTML is crawlable without JS execution.

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/grant/code/chroma-curve/board/planning/NOTE-0029_server-side-rendering-for-crawlers.md
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
17	  setCopyFormat: (format: CopyFormat) => void;
18	}

20	export const usePaletteStore = create<PaletteState>((set) => ({
21	  hue: 220,
22	  saturation: 0.5,
23	  sMod: 70,
24	  satMode: "endpoint",
25	  lMin: 0,
26	  lMax: 100,
27	  copyFormat: "hex",
28	  setHue: (hue) => set({ hue }),
29	  setSaturation: (saturation) => set({ saturation }),
30	  setSMod: (sMod) => set({ sMod }),
31	  setSatMode: (satMode) => set({ satMode }),
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-06 22:20:50Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/src/hooks/useDynamicFavicon.ts`

```text
1	import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";
2	import { usePaletteStore } from "../store/paletteStore";
3	
4	const SIZE = 32;
5	
6	function buildFaviconSvg(hue: number): string {
7	  return `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 ${SIZE} ${SIZE}">
8	  <defs>
9	    <linearGradient id="sat" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
10	      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="white"/>
11	      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="hsl(${hue}, 100%, 50%)"/>
12	    </linearGradient>
13	    <linearGradient id="val" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
14	      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="black" stop-opacity="0"/>
15	      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="black"/>
16	    </linearGradient>
17	    <clipPath id="clip"><rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}"/></clipPath>
18	    <filter id="shadow"><feDropShadow dx="0" dy="0" stdDeviation="1.5" flood-color="black" flood-opacity="0.5"/></filter>
19	  </defs>
20	  <g clip-path="url(#clip)">
21	    <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="url(#sat)"/>
22	    <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="url(#val)"/>
23	  </g>
24	  <rect width="${SIZE}" height="${SIZE}" fill="none" stroke="hsl(0,0%,20%)" stroke-width="1.5"/>
25	  <path d="M24 2 Q-2 16 24 30" fill="none" stroke="white" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#shadow)"/>
26	</svg>`;
27	}
28	
29	function renderSvgToPngHref(svg: string): Promise<string> {
30	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
31	    const img = new Image();
32	    const blob = new Blob([svg], { type: "image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8" });
33	    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
34	
35	    img.onload = () => {
36	      const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
37	      canvas.width = SIZE;
38	      canvas.height = SIZE;
39	      const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d")!;
40	      ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0, SIZE, SIZE);
41	      URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
42	      resolve(canvas.toDataURL("image/png"));
43	    };
44	
45	    img.src = url;
46	  });
47	}
48	
49	function setFavicon(href: string) {
50	  // Remove all existing icon links so browsers don't prefer a static fallback
51	  document
52	    .querySelectorAll<HTMLLinkElement>('link[rel="icon"]')
53	    .forEach((el) => el.remove());
54	
55	  const link = document.createElement("link");
56	  link.rel = "icon";
57	  link.type = "image/png";
58	  link.href = href;
59	  document.head.appendChild(link);
60	}
61	
62	export function useDynamicFavicon() {
63	  const hue = usePaletteStore((s) => s.hue);
64	  const pending = useRef(0);
65	
66	  useEffect(() => {
67	    const id = ++pending.current;
68	    const svg = buildFaviconSvg(hue);
69	
70	    renderSvgToPngHref(svg).then((href) => {
71	      // Drop stale renders if hue changed while we were rasterizing
72	      if (id !== pending.current) return;
73	      setFavicon(href);
74	    });
75	  }, [hue]);
76	}
77	
[result-id: r6]
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-06 22:21:08Z)**_

Good news: the codebase is pretty clean for this migration. Here are the actual concerns, ranked:

**Low risk (easy fixes):**

1. **`useDynamicFavicon`** — uses `document.createElement("canvas")`, `Image()`, and DOM manipulation. All inside a `useEffect` so it won't fire during SSG build, but the `Image` constructor and `URL.createObjectURL` don't exist in Node. Should be fine since `useEffect` doesn't run during SSR/SSG — just worth verifying.

2. **`useCopyToClipboard`** — references `navigator.clipboard`. Same story — event-driven, won't fire at build time.

3. **`export.ts`** — creates an anchor element for downloads. Only called on user action, no concern.

**Not a concern:**

- **Zustand store** — pure in-memory with static defaults, no `localStorage` persistence or `window` access at init time. SSG will render with the defaults cleanly.
- **Charts** — they're SVG-based (no canvas, no `getBoundingClientRect`), so they'll pre-render fine.
- **Framer Motion** — SSR-compatible; animations hydrate on the client.

**The one thing to actually think about:**

`vite-react-ssg` replaces `createRoot` with `react-dom/server` rendering during the build. If any module imported by `App.tsx` has **top-level side effects** that touch browser globals (not in a hook or handler), the build will crash. I didn't find any — all browser API usage is inside `useEffect` or event handlers — so this should be a clean migration.

Basically: no blockers. The main work is swapping out `main.tsx` entry point and adding the SSG plugin to `vite.config.ts`.

---

```md
# EPIC-0013: Add static site generation for crawler visibility

All page content is currently rendered client-side, so crawlers and LLMs see an empty shell. Add SSG via `vite-react-ssg` to pre-render the home page at build time.

## Context

- Single-page app, one route (`/`)
- Zustand store has static defaults — SSG captures a meaningful initial render
- Charts are SVG-based, no canvas concerns
- All browser API usage is inside `useEffect` or event handlers (no top-level side effects)
- See NOTE-0029 for initial research

## Tasks

- TASK-0047: Install vite-react-ssg and configure Vite plugin
- TASK-0048: Migrate main.tsx entry point to SSG
- TASK-0049: Audit and guard browser-only code paths for SSG safety
- TASK-0050: Validate build output contains pre-rendered HTML

```

```md
# TASK-0049: Audit and guard browser-only code paths for SSG safety

Verify that all browser API usage (`document`, `navigator`, `Image`, `URL.createObjectURL`) is safely inside `useEffect` or event handlers and won't execute during Node-based SSG build. Key files to check:

- `useDynamicFavicon.ts` — canvas rendering, DOM link manipulation
- `useCopyToClipboard.ts` — `navigator.clipboard`
- `export.ts` — anchor element creation for downloads
- `TargetSwatch.tsx` — `document.addEventListener` for drag

Add guards if any code path could fire at build time.
```
